prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetheart, a CPU is something they call the "brain" of the computer. It’s a tiny chip inside that does all the thinking and tells the computer what to do when you play games or watch your videos. You know how I sometimes follow a recipe to make cookies? The CPU follows instructions, sort of like a recipe, to help everything work properly. I don’t know all the little details, but without it, the computer wouldn’t know what to do at all. So, it’s very important, even if it’s just a little thing you can barely see!
